Items.Author: yourmdsarfaraj

Items.Title: 几何深度学习的简要介绍 | A gentle introduction to Geometric Deep Learning
Items.Description:
人工智能改变了我们的世界，智能系统是我们日常生活的一部分，它们正在颠覆各个领域的行业。

在所有人工智能学科中，深度学习是目前最热门的。 机器学习从业者成功地实施了深度神经网络 (DNN) 来解决许多科学领域的挑战性问题。

如今，汽车可以看到十字路口的繁忙程度，可以与想象中的哲学家进行愉快的交谈，或者我们可以简单地看着 AI 摧毁我们最喜欢的职业玩家。 在我们有足够的数据来训练模型之前，唯一的限制就是想象力。

从广义上讲，我们用来训练深度学习模型的数据属于两个主要领域：

  • 欧几里得数据：在多维线性空间中表示的数据，它遵循欧几里得假设。 示例是文本或表格数据（例如随时间变化的狗狗币价格）
  • 非欧几里得数据：更广泛地说，不服从欧几里得假设的数据（例如，在这些领域中，两点之间的距离不是直线）。 例如分子结构、社交网络交互或网格 3D 表面。


非欧几里得数据的例子。 在应用 AI 中，深度学习模型在处理欧几里得数据时效果很好，但在处理非欧几里得数据时却很困难。 这是一个巨大的问题，因为在所有科学领域都可以找到这种性质的数据！

幸运的是，在过去的几年里，ML从业者(❤️) 创造了几何深度学习这个术语，并开始将理论形式化以解决这个问题。 这个新兴的 AI 领域试图使用几何原理推广 DNN 以解决非欧几里得域。

为什么几何如此重要？

自希腊时代以来，欧几里得几何一直是研究几何性质的唯一学科。 即使是现在，欧几里得几何仍然是描述我们周围许多现象的有力工具。 正如我们之前看到的，它遵循简单的原则并且很容易理解：例如两点之间的距离是一条直线，或者所有直角都全等。

然而，在 19 世纪，许多数学家通过改变欧几里得的公设推导出替代几何。 他们还意识到，这些新几何形状非常擅长解释物体在不同空间中的交互方式。 例子包括罗巴切夫斯基的双曲几何或黎曼的椭圆几何。

不幸的是，所有这些不同的几何形状在多个独立的领域中都存在分歧。 当时一个合理的问题是：哪一个是真实的？

幸运的是，在 1872 年，Felix Klein 教授用他的名为Erlangen Program的工作解决了这个复杂的难题。 他提出了一个框架，通过使用不变性和对称性的概念来统一所有几何图形。


这个想法导致了定义几何的蓝图，只需指定数据所在的域以及数据必须尊重的对称性。

非欧几里得数据的深度学习

深度学习研究人员非常出色。 每天在Arxiv上发表的 ML 文章超过 100 篇。&text=That%20is%20about%2033%2C000%20papers%20per%20year.)！ 我们充斥着承诺比其他模型更好地解决问题的新模型，但我们没有统一的规则来定义能够在所有可能的数据域上概括其属性的 AI 架构。


非欧几里得数据会使事情变得复杂。 卷积神经网络(CNN) 是机器学习中最重要的算法之一。 因此，它们已被广泛用于解决计算机视觉问题。


卷积一词指的是一个非常简单的操作：在图像上滑动一个小矩阵（通常称为过滤器）并执行逐元素乘法，然后求和以产生另一个矩阵（见下面的 gif）。 通过此操作，可以从输入图像中提取低级特征，例如边缘、形状或亮点。


卷积示例

卷积之所以起作用，是因为我们使用二维数据结构来表示图像。 通过这种方式，可以使用卷积过滤器共享网络权重，并且无论输入图像的移位或失真如何都可以提取信息（对所有图像应用相同的过滤器）。 这是通过使用输入数据的几何形状来简化问题的第一个示例。

我们如何将这个想法形式化并扩展到其他领域？ 也许应用几何原理可以像过去一样帮助我们。

为什么学习非欧几里得数据很复杂？

不幸的是，非欧几里得数据遭受维数诅咒！ 为了更好地解释它，让我们考虑一个主要的 ML 应用程序：监督学习。 它的主要目标是通过使用训练数据逼近函数来学习解决任务。 由于通用逼近定理，我们知道我们甚至可以使用简单的神经网络来重现任何连续函数（达到任何所需的精度）。


神经网络是通用逼近器：只有一个隐藏层，它们可以表示阶跃函数的组合，允许以任意精度逼近任何连续函数。 不幸的是，事情在高维中变得复杂：我们需要指数数量的样本来逼近最简单的多维函数。


多维连续函数的逼近。 解决这个问题的一个常见方法是将高维数据投影到低维空间（例如使用PCA算法降维）但是，这并不总是可行的，因为我们可能会丢弃重要信息。


使用 PCA 可以减少尺寸，但它总是我们想要的吗？ 为了克服这个问题，我们可以使用输入数据的几何结构。 这种结构正式称为几何先验，它有助于形式化我们应该如何处理输入数据。

例如，将图像视为二维结构，而不是将像素的d维向量视为几何先验。 先验很重要，因为通过使用它们，可以限制我们可以拟合输入数据的函数数量，并使高维问题易于处理。

例如，通过考虑几何先验，可以识别以下函数：

  • 可以独立于任何班次（CNN）处理图像；
  • 可以独立于旋转处理投影在球体上的数据（球形 CNN ） ；
  • 可以独立于同构（图神经网络）处理图数据。

几何深度学习确定了我们的函数必须尊重的两个主要先验，以简化问题并将几何假设注入输入数据：

  1. 对称性：被保持对象不变的函数所尊重，它们必须是可组合的、可逆的，并且它们必须包含标识；
  2. 旋转是一个尊重等边三角形域中的对称性的函数。 这两个原则不是理论定义，而是源自最成功的深度学习架构（如 CNN、Deep Set 和 Transformers）。

    几何深度学习先验为我们提供了定义可以从任何数据中学习的深度学习架构的蓝图。 如果我们定义的函数类尊重这些属性，我们可以处理任何数据域。

    几何深度学习在图神经网络中的应用

    图是最灵活的数据表示。 现实世界中的对象通常与其他对象有关系或联系，将它们表示为图形是最直接的方法。


    血清素分子的图形表示（最快乐的🥳）。 我们可以看到像实体集合（节点）和它们之间的关系（边）这样的图。 如果节点之间存在方向关系，则该图称为Directed ，否则称为Undirected 。


    具有 4 个节点和 4 个边的简单有向图。

    在数学上，我们可以定义一个图： $$G = (V, E)$$ 其中 V 是节点的集合，E 是它们之间的边。

    为了简化符号，图也可以用邻接矩阵（A）表示，矩阵的元素表示图中的节点对是否相邻。


    令 ( x_i \in R^k ) 为节点 ( i ) 的特征向量。 我们可以将所有特征向量堆叠在一个形状为 (n * k) 的矩阵 $$X= (x_1, ..., x_n)^T $$ 中，其中 ( n ) 是特征节点的维度， ( k )是节点数。

    但是，通过这样做，我们指定了一个节点排序！ 这可能会导致很多问题，因为我们需要独立于同构来处理图数据。 例如，以下图表相同，但排列方式不同：


    让我们使用几何深度学习先验通过定义一类能够处理图数据的函数（图神经网络）来简化问题。 我们想要的是一种独立于节点组织处理图数据的方法：


    所有可能的节点组合都由称为排列的数学运算定义。 排列简单地由一个矩阵 (P) 定义，该矩阵可以左乘到特征矩阵 (X) 或邻接矩阵 (A)。


    将第二行更改为第三行的排列示例。

    鉴于这些定义，我们可以确定我们的函数必须尊重的两个主要属性：

    1. 排列不变性：应用排列不应该修改结果。 用数学术语（ f(PX, PAP^T)=f(X, A) ）
    2. 这两个属性与上面定义的第一个几何先验直接相关。 正如我们之前所讨论的，我们通过在像素邻居上使用卷积（更正式地说：使用局部等变处理层）解决了卷积神经网络中的类似问题（像素是否被平移并不重要）。

      事实证明，考虑到节点的邻域，可以在 GNN 中构建相同的置换不变层和等变层！ 最后的卷积是乘法，然后是和，我们可以定义类似的操作，引入一个能够处理节点邻居组的函数。

      让我们将节点 ( i ) 的邻居的集合 ( N_i ) 定义为 ( X_Ni={x_j: j N_i} )。 在这个设置中，我们可以简单地识别一个能够在本地处理所有节点的函数 ( g )：

      让我们可视化这些数学定义如何在图形处理中转化。 通过考虑节点 (x_b) 的局部邻域，可以使用聚合函数 (g(x_b, XN_b)) 创建隐藏表示 (h_b)。


      深度图神经网络是通过堆叠多个这些层构建的！ 使用此蓝图可以训练能够解决以下任务的 GNN：


      定义我们如何计算潜在表示，使用聚合函数 (g)（也称为隐藏层），我们可以有不同风格的 GNN：

      • 卷积 GNN ：它们使用邻居来计算当前节点的隐藏表示。 使用这种方法可以通过计算局部卷积来模拟 CNN。
      • 注意 GNN ：它们使用类似于 Transformer 模型的自我注意机制来学习每对连接节点之间的权重。
      • 消息传递 GNN ：它们通过在相邻节点之间交换信息来传播节点特征。

      可是等等！ 我们将在以下文章之一中进行更详细的介绍！ 不要忘记定期查看我们的博客🥳

      这里的主要内容可以用这句话来概括（我找不到原作者）：

      数据有形，形有义

      在传统的 ML 形状中，数据表示通常会丢失，几何深度学习告诉我们，我们可以通过考虑输入数据域的几何属性来利用 DL 模型的强大功能。

      结论

      在这篇小文章中，我们看到可以通过考虑底层数据几何结构来概括现代深度学习架构以在所有可能的数据域中学习。

      我们看到了几何深度学习如何帮助形式化我们的神经网络必须遵循的属性，以便利用输入数据的几何形状。

      我们完成了讨论在最常见的非欧几里得深度学习模型中应用的几何深度学习原理的文章：图神经网络。

      主要参考资料

      非常感谢Michael M. Bronstein教授和他的团队。

      有用的链接：



原文:

AI has changed our world, intelligent systems are part of our everyday life, and they are disrupting industries in all sectors.

Among all the AI disciplines, Deep Learning is the hottest right now. Machine Learning practitioners successfully implemented Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) to solve challenging problems in many scientific fields.

Nowadays, cars can see how busy a crossroad is, it’s possible to have pleasant conversations with imaginary philosophers, or we can simply have fun watching AIs destroying our favourite pro gamers. Until we have enough data to train models, the only limit is imagination.

In very broad terms, the data we use to train Deep Learning models belongs to two main domains:

  • Euclidean data : data represented in multidimensional linear spaces, it obeys Euclidean postulates. Examples are text or tabular data (e.g. Dogecoin price over time)
  • non-Euclidean data : in even broader terms, data that doesn’t obey Euclidean postulates (e.g. in these domains the distance between two points is not a straight line). Examples are Molecular Structures, Social Network Interactions, or Meshed 3D surfaces.


Examples of non-Euclidean Data. [source]

In applied AI, Deep Learning models work very well with Euclidean data, but they struggle when dealing with non-Euclidean data. This is a huge problem because it's common to find data of this nature in all the scientific fields!

Luckily, in the past few years, ML practitioners (❤️ ) coined the term Geometric Deep Learning and started formalizing the theory to solve this issue. This emerging AI field tries to generalize DNNs to tackle non-Euclidean domains using geometric principles.

Why is geometry so important?

Since the Greeks era, Euclidean Geometry has been the only discipline for studying geometric properties. Even now, Euclidean Geometry is a powerful tool to describe many of the phenomena around us. As we saw before, it follows simple principles and is straightforward to understand: e.g. the distance between 2 points is a straight line, or all the right angles are congruent.

However, during the 19th century, many mathematicians derived alternative geometries by changing Euclid's postulates. They also realized that these new geometries were very good at explaining how objects interact in different spaces. Examples include the hyperbolic geometry of Lobachevsky or the Elliptic geometry of Riemann.

Unfortunately, all these different geometries diverged in multiple independent fields. A legit question at the time was: which is the True One?

Luckily in 1872, professor Felix Klein solved this intricate puzzle with his work called Erlangen Program. He proposed a framework to unify all the geometries by using the concepts of Invariance and Symmetry.


Professor Felix Klein and his work. [source]

This idea led to a blueprint for defining a geometry by just specifying the domain where the data lives and what symmetries that data must respect.

The Erlangen Program had a significant influence on all the scientific fields, particularly in Physics, Chemistry and Math.

Deep Learning on non-Euclidean data

Deep Learning researchers are exceptional. Every day more than 100 ML articles are published on Arxiv.&text=That%20is%20about%2033%2C000%20papers%20per%20year.)! We are flooded with new models that promise to solve problems better than the others, but we don't have unifying rules to define AI architectures able to generalize their properties on all the possible data domains.


DNNs usually work with fixed-size structured inputs like vectors. Things get complicated with non-Euclidean data. [source]

Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) are one of the most important algorithms in Machine Learning. CNNs can acquire high level information from images, videos or other visual inputs. For this reason they have been widely used to solve Computer Vision problems.


CNNs are special Neural Networks able to extract information from visual inputs using Convolutions. [source]

The term Convolution refers to a very simple operation: slide a little matrix (usually called filter) over an image and perform an element-wise multiplication followed by a sum to produce another matrix (see gif below). With this operation is possible to extract low level features from the input image like edges, shapes or bright spots.


Example of Convolution

Convolutions work because we represent images using a 2-D data structure. In this way it’s possible to share the network weights using convolutional filters, and extract information regardless of shifts or distortions of the input images (the same filter is applied through all the image). This has been the first example of problem simplification by using the geometry of the input data.

How can we formalize and extend this idea for other domains? Maybe applying geometric principles can help us as it happened in the past.

Why is learning on non-Euclidean data complicated?

Unfortunately, non-Euclidean data suffers from dimensionality curse! To explain it better, let's consider one of the main ML applications: Supervised Learning. Its primary goal is to learn to solve a task by approximating a function using training data. (Functions are the result of DNNs training, we'll use the word function in the following section)

Thanks to the universal approximation theorem, we know that we can use even a simple Neural Network to reproduce any continuous function (to any desired accuracy).


Neural Networks are universal approximators: with just one hidden layer, they can represent combinations of step functions, allowing to approximate any continuous function with arbitrary precision. [Source]

Unfortunately, things get complicated in high dimensions: we need an exponential number of samples to approximate even the most straightforward multidimensional function.


Approximation of a continuous function in multiple dimensions. [source]

A common fix for this issue is to project high dimensional data to a low dimensional space (for example reducing dimension using the PCA algorithm) However, this is not always possible because we may discard important information.


With PCA it is possible to reduce dimensions, but is it always what we want? [source]

To overcome this problem, we can use the geometric structure of input data. This structure is formally called Geometric prior and it is useful to formalize how we should process the input data.

For example, considering an image as a 2-D structure instead that a d-dimensional vector of pixels is a Geometric prior. Priors are important because by using them, it's possible to restrict the number of functions that we can fit with the input data and make the high-dimensional problem tractable.

For example by considering geometric priors, it is possible to identify functions that:

  • Can process images independently of any shift (CNNs);
  • Can process data projected on spheres independently of rotations (Spherical CNNs);
  • Can process graph data independently of isomorphism (Graph Neural Networks).

Geometric Deep Learning identifies two main priors our functions must respect to simplify the problem and injecting geometric assumptions to input data:

  1. Symmetry : is respected by functions that leave an object invariant, they must be composable, invertible, and they must contain the identity;
  2. Scale Separation : the function should be stable under slight deformation of the domain.


Rotation is a function that respects Symmetry in the equilateral triangles domain. [source]


CNNs are robust to data deformation.

These two principles are not theoretical definitions but they are derived from the most successful Deep Learning architecture (like CNNs, Deep Set and Transformers).

The Geometric Deep Learning priors give us the blueprint to define Deep Learning architectures that can learn from any data. If the class of functions we define respect these properties we can tackle any data domain.

Geometric Deep Learning applied in Graph Neural Networks

Graphs are the most flexible data representation. Objects in the real world usually have relationships or connections with other objects, representing them as graphs is the most straightforward approach.


Graph representation of the Serotonin molecule ( the happiest 🥳 ). [source]

We can see graphs like a collection of entities (N odes ) and the relationships between them ( Edges ). If there is a directional relationship between nodes the graph is called Directed , otherwise is called Undirected.


Simple Directed Graph with 4 Nodes and 4 Edges.

Mathematically we can define a graph with: $$G = (V, E)$$ Where V is the set of nodes, and E are the edges between them.

To simplify the notation, a graph can be also represented with an adjacency matrix (A ), the elements of the matrix indicate whether pairs of nodes are adjacent or not in the graph.


3 graphs and their adjacency matrix. [source]

Let ( x_i \in R^k ) be the feature vector of the node ( i ). We can stack all the feature vectors in a matrix $$X= (x_1, ..., x_n)^T $$ of shape ( n * k ) , where ( n ) is the dimension of the feature node and ( k ) is the number of nodes.

However, by doing this we are specifying a node ordering! This can cause a lot of problems because we need to process graph data independently of isomorphism. For example the following graphs are the same but they are arranged differently:


[source]

Let’s use the Geometric Deep Learning priors to simplify the problem by defining a class of functions (Graph Neural Networks) able to process graph data. What we want is a way to process graph data independently of node organization:


[source]

All the possible node combinations are defined by a mathematical operation called Permutation. Permutations are simply defined by a matrix ( P ) that can be left-multiplied to the feature matrix ( X ) or to the adjacency matrix ( A ).


Example of permutation that changes the second row with the third.

Given these definitions we can identify two main properties our function must respect:

  1. Permutation Invariance : applying permutations shouldn’t modify the result. In mathematical terms ( f(PX, PAP^T)=f(X, A) )
  2. Permutation Equivariance : it doesn’t matter if we apply permutations before or after the function. ( f(PX, PAP^T)=Pf(X, A) )

These two properties are directly correlated to the first Geometric Prior defined above. As we discussed before, we solved a similar problem in Convolutional Neural Networks (it doesn't matter if pixels are translated) by using Convolutions on pixels neighbours (more formally: using local equivariant processing layers).

It turned out that is possible to build the same permutation Invariant and Equivariant layers in GNNs considering node’s neighbourhoods! Convolutions at the end are multiplications followed by a sum, we can define similar operations introducing a function able to process groups of nodes neighbours.

Let’s define the set ( N_i ) of the neighbours of node ( i ) as ( X_Ni={x_j: j N_i} ). In this setting we can simply identify a function ( g ) able to process locally all the nodes:

Let’s visualize how these mathematical definitions translate in graph processing. By considering the local neighbourhood of the node ( x_b ) is possible to create an hidden representation ( h_b ) using the aggregation function ( g(x_b, XN_b) ).


[source]

Deep Graph Neural Networks are built by stacking multiple of these layers! 🥳


[source]

Using this blueprint is possible to train GNNs able to solve the following tasks:


[source]

Defining how we compute the latent representations, using the aggregation function ( g ) (aka the hidden layers) we can have different flavours of GNNs:

  • Convolutional GNNs : they use the neighbours to compute the hidden representation of the current node. Using this approach is possible to mock CNNs by computing localized convolutions.
  • Attentional GNNs : they use a self attention mechanism similar to Transformer models to learn weights between each couple of connected nodes.
  • Message passing GNNs : they propagate node features by exchanging information between adjacent nodes.

But wait! ... This is only an introduction on GNNs. We will go in more detail in one of the following articles! Don't forget to check our blog regularly 🥳

The main takeaway here can be summarized with this quote (which I couldn't find the original author):

Data has shape, and shape has a meaning

In traditional ML shape is usually lost in data representation, Geometric Deep learning tells us that we can leverage the power of DL models by considering the geometrical properties of the input data domain.

Conclusions

In this small article we saw that it is possible to generalize modern Deep Learning architectures to learn in all possible data domains by considering the underlying data Geometric Structure.

We saw how Geometric Deep Learning can help formalize the properties our Neural Network must follow in order to exploit the geometry of input data.

We finished the article discussing the Geometric Deep Learning principles applied in the most common non-Euclidean Deep Learning model: Graph Neural Networks.

Main references

A big thanks to professor Michael M. Bronstein and his team.

Items.Title: 社区之星系列 | 社区人才编写使用教程！ | Community Star Series | 1 Don’t know how to use Apache DolphinScheduler? A community talent writes the usage tutorial!
Items.Description:
作者 | 欧阳涛，大数据开发工程师

顶级开源项目，类似于其他开源项目，从脚本运行和安装。

这些脚本都位于根目录的脚本文件夹中，并按以下顺序执行：

  • 查看启动脚本start-all.sh，可以找到四个最重要的启动服务的启动，分别是dolphinscheduler-daemon.sh start master-server/worker-server/alert-server/api-server
  • 紧接着，dolphinscheduler-daemon.sh 脚本循环遍历上述四个模块下的 bin/start.sh，如下图所示。

如下图： dolphinscheduler-daemon.sh start master-server 的执行会到master模块的src/main/bin，执行start.sh。 打开start.sh后，可以发现启动了一个MasterServer。 其他 Worker、Alert 和 API 模块的启动方式相同。

脚本流程到此结束，下面我们来介绍四个模块的主要用途：Master负责DAG任务切片，任务提交监控，监听其他Masters和Worker的健康状态； 如果你熟悉其他大数据项目，比如 Flink、Hdfs、Hbase 等，你会发现这些架构都差不多，比如 hdfs 被架构成 NameNode 和 WorkNode； 备注：

  • 运行脚本中有一个python-gateway-server模块，它是用python代码工作流编写的，不在本文讨论范围内，所以这次我忽略它，如果你详细了解这个模块，你最好转向社区。

  • 启动Alert 脚本执行alert-server 脚本下的Alert 模块，因为Alert 也是一个父模块，这里我不打算多讲alert-server。 相信经过Master和Worker的实现过程，Alert模块应该不难理解。

  • 另外，如果你是第一次了解DS，你会在Alert模块中发现一个alert-api，我想说明这个alert-api和前面提到的api-server是完全不相关的，api-server就是ApiApplicationServer启动api模块的脚本，负责整个DS的服务器逻辑。 同样，task模块下的task-api和alert-api只是职责相同，但处理的功能不同。

  • 更多关于警告模块的信息，请参考以下链接或咨询其他社区成员。

    下一章我会介绍DS、Master、Worker这两个最重要的模块，以及它们之间是如何通信的，敬请期待！

    加入社区

    有很多方法可以参与和为 DolphinScheduler 社区做出贡献，包括：

    文档、翻译、问答、测试、代码、文章、主题演讲等。

    我们假设第一个贡献的 PR（文档、代码）很简单，应该用来熟悉提交过程和社区协作风格。

    所以社区整理了以下适合新手的问题列表： https ://github.com/apache/dolphinscheduler/issues/5689

    非新手问题列表： https ://github.com/apache/dolphinscheduler/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22volunteer+wanted%22

    如何参与投稿： https ://dolphinscheduler.apache.org/en-us/community/development/contribute.html

    官网： https://dolphinscheduler.apache.org/

    邮件列表：dev@ dolphinscheduler @apache.org

    推特： @dolphinschedule

    松弛： https://s.apache.org/dolphinscheduler-slack

    贡献者指南： https://dolphinscheduler.apache.org/en-us/community/index.html

    您对项目的 Star 很重要，不要犹豫，为 Apache DolphinScheduler 点亮 Star ❤️



原文:

Author | Ouyang Tao, Big Data Development Engineer

Apache DolphinScheduler (hereinafter referred to as DS) is a distributed and easily scalable visual DAG workflow task scheduling system, dedicated to solving the intricate dependencies in the data processing, thus making it be used out of the box in the data processing. The top open source project, similar to other open-source projects, runs and installs from a script.

The scripts are all located in the script folder in the root directory and are executed in the following order:

  • Check the start script start-all.sh, you can find the start of the four most important startup services, respectively dolphinscheduler-daemon.sh start master-server/worker-server/alert-server/api-server
  • The dolphinscheduler-daemon.sh script will first execute the dolphinscheduler-env.sh script, which serves to introduce the environment, including the Hadoop, Spark, Flink, Hive environments, etc. As DS needs to schedule these tasks, if these environments are not introduced, the execution will not succeed even if the scheduling is completed.
  • Immediately afterward, the dolphinscheduler-daemon.sh script loops through bin/start.sh under the four modules mentioned above, as shown in the following image.

As shown in the picture below: the execution of dolphinscheduler-daemon.sh start master-server will go to the master module’s src/main/bin and execute start.sh. After opening start.sh, you can find that a MasterServer is started. And the other Worker, Alert and API modules are started as the same.

This is the end of the scripting process, and we will now describe the main purpose of the four modules: Master is responsible for DAG task slicing, task submission monitoring, and listening to the health status of other Masters and Workers; Worker is responsible for task execution; Alert aims to the alert service; API works for the business logic of DS, i.e. project management, resource management, security management, etc. as seen on the web.

If you are familiar with other big data projects, such as Flink, Hdfs, Hbase, etc., you will find that these architectures are similar, such as hdfs is architected as the NameNode and WorkNode; Hbase is architected as the HMasterServer and HRegionServer; Flink adopts the architecture as JobManager and TaskManager, etc., if you can master these frameworks, I think the mastery of DS will be easier.

Master and Worker are started through SpringBoot, and the objects created are also hosted by Spring, if you use Spring regularly, I think you will understand DS much easier than other open-source projects.

Notes：

  • There is a python-gateway-server module in the run script, which is written in python code workflow and is not in the scope of this article, so I ignore it this time, if you understand this module in detail, you’d better turn to the community.

  • Start Alert script executes the Alert module under the alert-server script, because Alert is also a parent module, I do not intend to talk about alert-server much here. I believe that after going through the implementation process of Master and Worker, Alert module should not be difficult to understand.

  • In addition, if you know about DS for the first time, you will find an alert-api in the Alert module, I want to specify that this alert-api and the aforementioned api-server are totally unrelated, api-server is the ApiApplicationServer script to start the api module, and responsible for the server logic of the entire DS. The api-server is the ApiApplicationServer script that starts the api module and is work for the business logic of the whole DS, while alert-api is the plug-in interface of the spi that is responsible for alerting, and you can open the alert-api module to find that the code inside is all interfaces and definitions and does not deal with any logic. Similarly, the task-api and alert-api under the task module just have the same responsibilities, but deal with different functions.

  • DS is all managed by SpringBoot, so if you have never worked with SpringBoot or Spring, you can refer to the following URLs and other relevant information on the web. https://spring.io/quickstart

Know more about the warning module, please refer to the link below or consult other community members.

https://dolphinscheduler.apache.org/zh-cn/blog/Hangzhou_cisco.html

The official website of the Apache DolphinScheduler project can be found at: https://github.com/apache/dolphinscheduler

In the next chapter, I will introduce the two most important modules of DS, Master, and Worker, and how they communicate with each other, stay tuned!

Join the Community

There are many ways to participate and contribute to the DolphinScheduler community, including:

Documents, translation, Q&A, tests, codes, articles, keynote speeches, etc.

We assume the first PR (document, code) to contribute to be simple and should be used to familiarize yourself with the submission process and community collaboration style.

So the community has compiled the following list of issues suitable for novices: https://github.com/apache/dolphinscheduler/issues/5689

List of non-newbie issues: https://github.com/apache/dolphinscheduler/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22volunteer+wanted%22

How to participate in the contribution: https://dolphinscheduler.apache.org/en-us/community/development/contribute.html

GitHub Code Repository: https://github.com/apache/dolphinscheduler

Official Website：https://dolphinscheduler.apache.org/

MailList：dev@dolphinscheduler@apache.org

Twitter：@dolphinschedule

YouTube：https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCmrPmeE7dVqo8DYhSLHa0vA

Slack：https://s.apache.org/dolphinscheduler-slack

Contributor Guide：https://dolphinscheduler.apache.org/en-us/community/index.html

Your Star for the project is important, don’t hesitate to lighten a Star for Apache DolphinScheduler ❤️

Items.Title: 为什么我喜欢学习？ | Why do I like to learn?
Items.Description:
我想我不能停止提高我的知识。 但为什么？

如果这实际上导致我不满意，为什么我需要越来越多地改进？

我什么时候对我所知道的感到满意？

问题是我总是觉得自己还处于起步阶段。 尽管我已经走过了很多路：这就像我的知识只是一点点。

我对自己在做什么以及周围的环境没有清晰的认识。

这就是问题。 如此清晰是可以达到的，还是我总是被限制在近似的领域？

我认为最后一个是正确的。

我所知道的总是对现实的近似，我必须接受这一点。

改进总是必要的，但仅此是不够的。 我想念的是有意义的联系。 如果我没有做到这一点，我会一直不满意，改进也救不了我。



原文:

I think I can't stop improving my knowledge. But why?

Why do I need to improve, more and more if this, in fact, leads me to dissatisfaction?

When do I feel satisfaction about what I know?

The problem is that I always feel as if I'm at the beginning. Even though I have covered a lot of roads: it's such as my knowledge is just a little.

I don't have a clear understanding of what I'm doing and the environment surrounding me.

This is the problem. Is it such clearness reachable or am I confined always in the field of approximation?

I think the last is the correct one.

What I know is always an approximation of reality and I have to make peace with this.

Improvement is always necessary but it alone is not enough. What I miss are meaningful connections. And If I don't achieve this, I will be always dissatisfied and improvement won't save me.

Items.Title: 紧凑型变电站——未来能源的关键 | Compact Substation - The Key to Future Energy
Items.Description:
能源行业的转型不仅仅是分布式发电和微电网。 它是关于数字化、人工智能和物联网 (IoT) 的。 事实上，许多新技术开始融合到一个单一的融合生态系统中，我们称之为数字能源网络。 能源网正在从大型集中式发电厂转向更小、更分布式的智能系统，这些系统可以监控和控制无论在哪里产生或消耗的电力。 随着住宅用户和工业用户对电力需求的推动，变电站解决方案必须足够紧凑以适应狭小空间，但必须具备在有限空间内实现可靠高压切换所需的所有基本组件。 紧凑型变电站可以与其他设备相结合，例如微型发电箱 (M^3 B) 模块化解决方案，可以远程监控通用外壳中的发电机和开关设备。

什么是紧凑型变电站？

紧凑型变电站是传统变电站的更小、更便宜、现成的模块化版本。 它专为需要将变电站安装到小空间（例如零售店后面或车库顶部）的客户而设计。 它按照与传统变电站相同的安全标准建造，但占地面积更小，成本更低。 紧凑型变电站具有低压、中压和高压切换能力。 它按照与传统变电站相同的安全标准建造，但占地面积更小，成本更低。 它拥有断路器、变压器、低压开关设备、控制室和服务客户​​所需的同级别设备。 它设计为模块化且易于扩展。

需要紧凑型变电站

尽管大型传统变电站非常适合其传统角色，但很明显，现有技术无法应对正在出现的不断发展的分布式系统以及由此带来的一些挑战。 由于已安装的变电站基础将在未来几十年保持不变，因此需要新一代更小、更模块化和更具成本效益的变电站解决方案。

为什么要建造紧凑型变电站？

我们相信，能源行业的转型将导致更加分散的电网和更多分布式发电：屋顶太阳能电池板、农村地区的风电场、偏远地区的柴油发电机以及城市的微电网。 这种转变将需要更小、更模块化的变电站以及传感器、通信系统、软件和其他技术的生态系统。 这是许多世界上最大的公用事业公司正在朝着这个方向发展的方向。

那么，紧凑型变电站中有什么？

构成紧凑型变电站的主要部件有四个： - 低压开关设备和控制装置 - 变电站的这一部分位于主断路器内部。 结论
能源行业的转型不仅仅是分布式发电和微电网。 它是关于数字化、人工智能和物联网 (IoT) 的。 事实上，许多新技术开始融合到一个单一的融合生态系统中，我们称之为数字能源网络。 能源网正在从大型集中式发电厂转向更小、更分布式的智能系统，这些系统可以监控和控制无论在哪里产生或消耗的电力。 随着住宅用户和工业用户对电力需求的推动，变电站解决方案必须足够紧凑以适应狭小空间，但必须具备在有限空间内实现可靠高压切换所需的所有基本组件。



原文:

The transformation of the energy industry is not just about distributed generation and micro-grids. It’s about digitization, artificial intelligence, and Internet of Things (IoT). In fact, many of these new technologies are beginning to merge into a single convergence eco-system that we call the Digital Energy Network. The energy grid is moving away from large, centralized power plants and towards smaller, more distributed smart systems that monitor and control electricity wherever it’s generated or consumed. With demand for electricity being driven by residential customers as much as industrial ones, substation solutions must be compact enough to fit in small spaces but have all the essential components needed for reliable high-voltage switching in a confined space. The compact substation can be combined with other equipment like Micro-Generation In A Box (M^3 B) modular solutions that enable remote monitoring of generators and switchgear in a common enclosure.

What is a Compact Substation?

A compact substation is a smaller, less expensive, off-the-shelf modular version of the traditional substation. It’s designed for customers who need to fit a substation into a small space such as behind a retail store or on top of a garage. It’s built to the same safety standards as a conventional substation but has a smaller footprint and lower cost. A compact substation has low-voltage, medium-voltage and high-voltage switching capability. It’s built to the same safety standards as a conventional substation but has a smaller footprint and lower cost. It has breakers, transformers, low-voltage switchgear, a control room and the same level of equipment needed to serve customers. It’s designed to be modular and easily expandable.

The Need for a Compact Substation

Although large, conventional substations are well-suited to their traditional role, it has become clear that the existing technology is ill-equipped for the evolving, distributed system that is emerging and some of the challenges this poses. Since the installed base of substations will remain in place for decades to come, there is a need for a new generation of smaller, more modular, and more cost-effective substation solutions.

Why Build a Compact Substation?

We believe the transformation of the energy industry will result in a more decentralized grid with more distributed generation: solar panels on rooftops, wind farms in rural areas, diesel-fired generators at remote locations, and micro-grids in cities. This shift will require smaller, more modular substations and an ecosystem of sensors, communication systems, software and other technology. This is the direction in which many of the world’s largest utility companies are moving.

So, what’s in a compact substation?

There are four main components that make up a compact substation: - Low-voltage switchgear and controls - This part of the substation is located inside the main breaker. - Medium-voltage switchgear and controls - Located on a common wall adjacent to the low-voltage switchgear and controls. - High-voltage switchgear and controls - Located on a common wall adjacent to the medium-voltage switchgear. - Monitoring and control room. - Essential for visualizing the full range of operations and functions.

Conclusion
The transformation of the energy industry is not just about distributed generation and micro-grids. It’s about digitization, artificial intelligence, and Internet of Things (IoT). In fact, many of these new technologies are beginning to merge into a single convergence eco-system that we call the Digital Energy Network. The energy grid is moving away from large, centralized power plants and towards smaller, more distributed smart systems that monitor and control electricity wherever it’s generated or consumed. With demand for electricity being driven by residential customers as much as industrial ones, substation solutions must be compact enough to fit in small spaces but have all the essential components needed for reliable high-voltage switching in a confined space.

Items.Title: 流行的测试自动化框架：类型、优势和测试工具 | Popular Test Automation Frameworks: Types, Benefits & Testing Tools
Items.Description:
西蒙丹尼尔斯

发表于手动测试一直过时，企业找到了新的替代方案。

事实上，这就是手动测试背后的故事。

您可能知道为什么手动测试没有受到关注，因为它是一个耗时、乏味的过程，并且需要对人力资源进行大量投资。

但是大多数企业仍然一无所知，并且拒绝在测试中实施自动化。 但如果他们部署测试自动化，它可以帮助他们降低劳动力成本、节省时间并提供精确的结果。

这样想； 程序员通过避免使用机器代码来使用编程语言来节省时间。 同样，测试人员可以利用测试自动化框架，绕过原始编程步骤，加快测试速度。

让我们深入研究自动化测试并了解框架的来龙去脉。

什么是测试自动化框架？
测试自动化框架是一组协议或指南，不依赖于单个流程或工具。 它结合了许多工具、实践和功能，包括可重用的模块、测试数据和库，它们协同工作以帮助 QA 专业人员有效地执行测试过程。

指南可以包括• 编码标准 • 测试数据处理过程 • 对象存储库 • 有关访问外部资源的信息

但是，这些指南具有灵活性，因为它取决于设计者如何将它们应用到行动中。 但是拥有一个有组织的框架可以帮助他们不错过相关步骤。

测试自动化框架的好处
当然，利用自动化架构框架的力量将帮助您的团队提高他们的测试效率和速度。 此外，它还有助于提高测试准确性，降低测试维护成本，并大大降低与之相关的整体风险。 以下是测试自动化框架的直观优势。

代码可重用性
该框架结合了相关的代码数据以成功地执行自动化测试，并且它可以重用于其他未来的框架。 最好的是它不需要手动重新排列或插入代码

低成本
测试自动化框架提供的主要好处是它们已经有一套给定的规则； 因此，测试用例是以合理的成本生成的。 此外，代码被无数次重复使用； 因此，设计涉及新功能的测试用例将在更短的时间内完成，成本也合理。

测试自动化框架符合规则和指南； 因此，在初始阶段就实现了最大覆盖。 如果该过程不成功，则可以通过较小的更改来重做自动化框架。 但它不需要更改数据，因此不需要团队的繁琐工作。

提高效率
由于标准化，测试自动化框架能够提高生产力。 它提供了最佳的测试覆盖率，因为代码从一开始就始终如一地执行。

你应该知道的自动化测试框架的类型
在讨论了测试自动化框架及其好处之后，让我们探索框架的范围。 下面解释了一些常见类型的自动化测试框架 -

该框架允许测试小型应用程序，通常被称为“记录和播放”框架。 它通过不编写用于创建函数的代码来帮助测试人员。

在这个框架中，步骤是按顺序写下来的。 测试人员可以记录每个步骤，包括用户输入、导航和能够自动播放脚本以执行测试的检查点。

优点• 无需编写自定义代码即可轻松进行测试。 缺点• 如果数据被更改，则需要修改脚本，因为该框架不可重用。

它是自动化测试框架之一，其中测试脚本将根据客户的需要通过将整个应用程序分成更小的模块来按模块创建。

测试人员可以通过将应用程序划分为不同的部分、功能或测试单元来利用其优势。 之后，将为每个部分创建测试脚本并集成以在层次结构中创建更大的测试。 主要目的是创建一个抽象层，允许在特定部分进行更改而不会干扰整个模块。

优点• 它允许在不影响整个模块的情况下更改单个测试脚本。

缺点• 它不允许利用数据集的大小，因为数据仍然是硬编码的。 数据驱动框架** 测试人员可以轻松地从文本、CSV、Excel 文件、DAO 对象和 ODBC 源等外部文件中读取数据。

它可以无缝地消除硬编码脚本的问题，正如在线性或模块化测试自动化框架中所看到的那样。 它可以帮助测试人员使用大量数据集多次测试应用程序的相同功能或特性。

优点• 无缝的多个数据集测试执行。 因此，如果对测试脚本进行任何更改，数据不会影响。

缺点• 使用此框架需要一位经验丰富且精通多种编程语言的测试人员。

你知道即使是非技术人员也能理解这个框架吗？ 懂简单英语的测试人员可以轻松创建测试用例并分析和理解它们。

此外，该框架可以创建一个生态系统，业务分析师、测试人员和开发人员可以轻松积极地参与其中。

优点• 它允许轻松创建测试规范。 缺点


每个功能或方法使用表格格式来表征关键字或动作作品。 它根据excel表中提到的关键字利用自动化测试脚本。 即使是具有较少编程专业知识的测试人员也可以执行测试脚本。

关键字是脚本的直观部分，它定义了执行 GUI 应用程序测试的多个操作。 它可以标记为单击、登录、单击链接或验证链接。

优点• 这是一个需要最少脚本知识的框架。 缺点• 该框架在设置过程中需要很高的初始投资。

混合框架是测试自动化框架的组合，可以利用彼此的优势来管理特定的测试环境。

每个应用程序都是独特且不同的，因此测试方法也应该如此。 为自动化测试建立一个灵活而健壮的框架是至关重要的。 混合结构可以更好地适应以获得最佳测试结果。

优点
缺点
因此，它们可以增强自动化工作。

机器人框架是一种基于 python 的、关键字驱动的方法，可以轻松执行测试。 它还能够测试 FTP、Android、MongoDB、Appium 和许多库，包括 WebDriver 库、Selenium 和其他有用的工具，这些工具使开源变得更加容易。

它是包含 Node.js 的自动化测试框架之一，并结合了本机和 Web 应用程序的测试运行器和自动化测试。 它还能够在 Chrome Devtools 和 Webdriver 协议上运行。 它由各种易于设置的自动化插件组成。

它具有自动化数据格式或消息传递协议的集成测试的能力。 您还可以将 Citrus 与 Selenium 集成以验证后端进程并与用户界面很好地互连。

它是著名的开源测试自动化架构框架之一，由于其跨浏览器和跨平台功能，可以作为大多数测试工具的基础。 不仅如此，它还具有支持多种编程语言的巨大潜力，包括 Ruby、Java、PHP、Python 等。当测试人员需要编写测试脚本来满足各种复杂性时，他们更喜欢这个框架。

它是一种行为驱动的开发工具，具有跨平台功能，可以极大地帮助测试人员为不同的 Web 应用程序编写验收测试。 简单快速的设置执行允许在测试中重用代码。 最令人难以置信的功能之一是它可以在一个最新文档中启用测试文档和规范上传。 它支持 Scala、Groovy 等编程语言。 网络、PHP 和 python。

前进的道路
当然，测试自动化框架是自动化测试的好方法。 测试框架提供的指南和协议可提供更快的缺陷检测和最小的人为干扰，并且可以重用测试脚本。

但是没有明确的赢家，每个框架都有自己的优势，可以根据客户的需求进行选择。 虽然，需要额外的研究来了解框架的最佳适用性。

原文:

simonedaniels

Posted on May 23

Manual testing is kept on obsoleting, and enterprises find a new alternative to it.

Indeed, that's the story behind manual testing.

You might know why manual testing is not getting the spotlight because it is a time-consuming, a tedious process and asks for a huge investment in human resources.

But most businesses are still ignorant and resist implementing automation in testing. But if they deploy testing automation, it can help them cutting-down labor costs, save time, and offer precise results.

Think this way; Programmers rescue their time by using programming language by avoiding machine code. Similarly, testers can leverage test automation frameworks, circumvent the raw programming steps, and speed up testing.

Let's dive into testing automation and understand the ins and outs of the frameworks.

What is a Test Automation Framework?
The test automation framework is a set of protocols or guidelines and doesn't rely on a single process or tool. It has incorporated many tools, practices, and functions, including reusable modules, test data, and libraries that work together to help QA professionals to perform the testing process effectively.

The guidelines can include • Coding standards • Test-data handling processes • Object repositories • Information regarding accessing external resources

• Test results storing processes

However, these guidelines have flexibility as it depends on the designer how to apply them to action. But having an organized framework helps them not to miss the relevant step.

Benefits of the test automation framework
Certainly, harnessing the power of automation architecture frameworks will help your team boost their testing efficiency and speed. In addition, it also helps in enhancing test accuracy, lowers test maintenance costs, and substantially reduces the overall risks involved with it. Here are the intuitive benefits of test automation frameworks.

Code Reusability
The framework incorporates the relevant code data to perform the automation test successfully, and it can reuse for other future frameworks. The best thing is that it doesn't require manually rearranging or inserting the code

Low cost
The key benefit offered by the test automation frameworks is that they already have a given set of rules; therefore, the test cases are generated at a reasonable cost. Furthermore, the codes are reused innumerable times; hence designing the test cases that involve new features will be done in less time and reasonable cost.

*Less Manual Interference *
The test automation frameworks comply with the rules and guidelines; therefore, the maximum coverage is accomplished at the initial stage. The automated frameworks can be redone with minor changes if the process doesn't succeed. But it doesn't require altering the data and hence requires no cumbersome efforts from the team.

Enhanced Efficiency
Due to standardization, test automation frameworks are capable of boosting productivity. It delivers optimum test coverage because the code is executed consistently from the start.

Types of automation testing frameworks that you should know
After discussing the test automation framework and its benefits, let's explore the range of frameworks. Some of the common types of automation testing frameworks are explained below-

1. Linear Automation framework
This framework allows for testing small applications and is popularly known as the "Record and playback" framework. It helps the testers by not writing the code for creating functions.

In this framework, the steps write down sequentially. The tester can record each step, including user input, navigation, and checkpoints capable of playing the script automatically to carry out the test.

Pros • Hassle-free testing as no demand for writing custom code. • Quickly generate scripts with easy recording. • Easy to understand as all steps are written sequentially.

• With its "Record and playback" feature, no need to plan substantially.

Cons • It requires modifying the script if data is altered because this framework isn't reusable.

• Maintenance is a big issue because it demands a plethora of rework.

2. Modular based testing framework
It is one of the automation testing frameworks in which the test scripts will get created module-wise by fragmenting the entire application into smaller modules as per clients' needs.

The testers can leverage its advantages by dividing the application into different sections, functions, or units for the test. After that, test scripts will be created for each part and integrated to create larger tests in a hierarchy. The main purpose is to create an abstraction layer that allows changes in a particular section without disturbing the whole module.

Pros • It allows altering the individual test script without disturbing the whole module.

• The test scripts are reusable, due to which creating test cases requires less effort.

Cons • It doesn't allow the leveraging magnitude of data sets as the data is still hard-coded. • For setting up this framework, knowledge of programming is needed.

**

  1. Data-driven framework** The tester can easily read the data from external files such as text, CSV, Excel files, DAO objects, and ODBC sources.

It can seamlessly wipe out the problem of the hard-coded script, as has been seen in linear or modular test automation frameworks. It helps testers test the same function or feature of an application as many times as testers want with numerous data sets.

Pros • Seamless multiple data sets testing execution. • It can reduce the number of scripts because of the quick testing of multiple scenarios with varying data.

• This framework can avoid hard-coded data; therefore, the data doesn't affect if do any changes in the test scripts.

Cons • Utilizing this framework requires a highly-experienced tester with proficiency in many programming languages.

• Setting up this framework requires a considerable amount of time.

4. Behavior-driven testing Framework
Did you know even a non-technical person can understand this framework? The tester who knows the simple English language can create test cases and analyze and understand them without trouble.

In addition, this framework can create an ecosystem in which business analysts, testers, and developers can participate easily and actively.

Pros • It allows the easy creation of test specifications. • It doesn't require technical language competency.

Cons


• Working with a client requires a dedicated team of developers.

5. Keyword-Driven Framework
A table format is used for each function or method to characterize keywords or action works. It leverages the automation test scripts according to the keywords mentioned in the excel sheet. Even a tester with less programming expertise can also perform the test scripts.

The keywords are the intuitive part of the script that defines multiple actions to carry out the test of the GUI application. It can be labeled as click, login, click link, or verify link.

Pros • It is a framework that requires minimal scripting knowledge. • Ease of code reusability

• The test scripts are written independently in reference to the software under test.

Cons • The framework requires a high initial investment during its setup.

• It needs top-notch technical expertise.

6. Hybrid Testing Framework
The hybrid framework is a combination of test automation frameworks that can leverage the benefits of each other to manage the particular test environment.

Each application is unique and different, and therefore the testing methodologies should be as well. Establishing a flexible and robust framework for automated testing is critical. A hybrid structure can be more adaptable to attain the best test results.

Pros
• It is one of the test automation frameworks that can harness the benefits of all associated frameworks.

Cons
• The tests are completely scripted; therefore, they can enhance the automation effort.

*5 Popular Test Automation Tools *

1. Robot Framework
Robot framework is a python-based, keyword-driven approach to performing the test easily. It can also be capable of testing the FTP, Android, MongoDB, Appium, and many libraries, including WebDriver library, Selenium, and rest helpful tools that make the open-source much easier.

2. WebdriverIO
It is one of the automation test frameworks that includes Node.js and incorporates the test runner and automation tests for native and web apps. It is also capable of running on the Chrome Devtools and Webdriver protocols. It consists of a variety of automation plugins that do the setup easy.

3. Citrus
It has the ability to automate integration tests for data format or messaging protocol. Citrus test automation framework can be exceptionally suited for test messaging integration for all the messaging transport, including HTTP, JMS, SOAP, and REST. You can also integrate Citrus with Selenium to authenticate the back-end processes and well interconnect with the user interface.

4. Selenium
It is one of the renowned open-source test automation architecture frameworks that can act as a base for most testing tools because of its cross-browser and cross-platform functionality. More than that, it has the immense potential to support diverse programming languages, including Ruby, Java, PHP, Python, etc. The testers prefer this framework when they need to write test scripts to meet a spectrum of complexities.

5. Cucumber
It is a behavior-driven development tool with cross-platform functionalities that can incredibly help testers write acceptance tests for different web apps. The easy and quick setup execution allowed for to reuse of the code in the tests. One of the most incredible features is that it can enable test documentation and specification uploads in one up-to-date document. It supports programming languages like Scala, Groovy,. Net, PHP, and python.

The way forward
Certainly, test automation frameworks are a great way for automation testing. The testing frameworks offer guidelines and protocols that provide quicker defect detection and minimum human interference and can reuse the test scripts.

But there is no clear winner, and every framework has its own benefits and can be chosen according to the client's needs. Although, additional research is necessary to understand the best suitability of the framework.

*Source: *https://www.zenesys.com/blog/popular-test-automation-frameworks

我将创建一个路线图🧭成为加密爱好者💗因为是否能够喜欢它的想法是最重要的。

这取决于你的想法🤔

专注于思想。 我不追逐代币的价格。
  • 介绍加密货币的思想，易于阅读

    • 如果你谷歌它，你可以找到很多广告。 它会告诉你“你必须买这个硬币！！！”

    所以，当你在那个时候，我会告诉你“不错的尝试！ ”👍

    我将在未来更新此路线图文章。

Items.Title: 对自己太苛刻 | To Be too harsh with yourself
Items.Description:
多梅尼科·里佐

发表于有时，我出于不诚实的原因参与活动。 我这样做是因为我的自我、我的成功或我的收入。

所以，与此同时，我宣布要改善世界，其实，我是在追求自己的利益。

所以，当我意识到自己行为的这方面时，我会感到没有动力，我认为自己是个骗子。

然后，我保证不再这样做，但不可避免地我再次陷入同样的​​错误，并且有同样的无动机的骗子基地过程。

这是否意味着我没有进步？ 当然不是。 只是改进的道路不是线性的。 我们有起有落，我们必须重复错误，最重要的是，我们必须不放弃。

我们真正的愿望应该在未来出现，我们将被揭示为我们是什么。 这一次我们应该决定是否改变以及如何改变。

Twyttler - Organize Favourite Tweets & Bookmarks! Twyttler - 整理最喜欢的推文和书签！ - - May 22 5月22日



原文:

Domenico Rizzo

Posted on May 23

Sometimes, I get involved in activities for not pure honest reasons. I do them because of my ego, my success, or my earnings.

So, meanwhile, I declare to want to improve the world, in the fact, I'm pursuing my own interests.

So, when I become aware of this aspect of my behavior then I feel unmotivated, I see myself as a liar.

Then, I promise to not do it anymore but inevitably I fall into the same mistakes again and have the same unmotivate liar-base process.

Does this mean I'm not improving? Of course, not. Only that the path toward improvements is not linear. We have ups and downs, We have to repeat mistakes, and above all, we have to not give up.

Our genuine desires should emerge in the future and we will be revealed for what we are. And this time we should decide if to change or not and how.

Twyttler - Organize Favourite Tweets & Bookmarks! - May 22
Items.Author: willypuzzle

Items.Title: 构建事物并学习。 你怎么？ | Build Things And Learn. How Do You?
Items.Description:
一位朋友和同事最近问我如何选择业余项目。 我不太确定如何回答这个问题，因为我从来没有考虑过我应该做些什么作为副业。 它总是更多地是关于我想做的事情。
我最近的项目是通过 Raspberry Pi（后来是 ESP8266）控制的自定义 LED 安装，因为我想了解 API 的生产者方面，我将如何在内部和公共网络上提供控制，如何实现使其可被发现并记录下来。 我日常工作中基本上不需要的东西（Postman 的开发人员关系项目经理），但这对我理解挑战有很大帮助。

我就是这样学习的。

它的另一部分是焊接、木工、面包板——这是在电脑屏幕前工作的一种受欢迎的替代方式。 它强调了我对实际学习的情感依恋，从而提高了我的生产力。

那你呢？ 你怎么学得最好？



原文:

A friend and colleague recently asked me how I choose side projects. I wasn't quite sure how to answer this, because I never thought too much about what I should do as a side thing. It's always been more about what I want to do.
My recent project, a custom LED installation controlled through a Raspberry Pi (later an ESP8266), was brought to life because I wanted to learn about the producer side of APIs, how I would make controls available internally and on a public network, how to make it discoverable and document it. Things that I don't essentially need on my day job (a Program Manager on Developer Relations at Postman), but that would help me a lot with understanding challenges.

That's how I learn.

The other part to it, the soldering, woodworking, breadboarding - that's a welcome alternation to working in front of a computer screen. And it emphasises how I emotionally attach to the actual learning and thus increases my productivity.

What about you? How do you learn best?

Items.Title: 每个 HR 软件必须具备的主要功能 | Top Features Every HR Software Must Have
Items.Description:
人力资源部门可能是一个复杂的管理部门。 在招聘、日程安排、福利、交叉合规等之间导航有许多责任。

如果员工希望看到旧的加薪会发生什么？ 任何人如何获得有关他们的假期或福利的信息？

如果没有正确的技术，这对所有相关人员来说都是一件麻烦事。

这就是为什么专门的人力资源软件变得如此受欢迎的原因。 各种规模的公司都使用这些工具来同时提高效率和员工体验。

下面提到的是一些重要的软件功能：

该技术可以自动化和优化对等角色的数据分析，并提出适当的薪水。 通过易于准备的模板和招聘跟进，人力资源软件自动化招聘模块加快了招聘程序。

可以使用 Employee Portal HR 软件组织和存储财务数据。 这样的门户允许授权用户实时访问他们的信息，通过消除监督此类活动的需要来节省人力资源时间。

工作流程使公司能够自动化大多数扼杀人力资源管理生产力的重复性常规程序。 让所有参与者了解情况。

申请人跟踪系统取代了壁橱中存放数百份简历的巨大文件夹。 它可以自动寻找人员，使您能够评估各种招聘渠道的效率，并确定哪些工作足够好以及应该将更多的精力和资源集中在哪里。

通过让 KPA 和 KRA 负责，HR 软件有效地简化了评估过程。 它提供了每个员工的出勤和准时更透明和公平的输出。

随着技术和流程的发展，每位员工都必须升级他们的技能。 人力资源软件有助于识别这种技能差距，帮助管理员工的培训需求，并跟踪与培训和发展相关的可观成本。

八、员工福利管理

人力资源专家可以使用该系统创建计划、设置资格条件、管理员工薪酬和晋升，以及在员工收入的基础上向员工支付或存款。 它还包括自助开放注册和补偿支出的会计集成。

人力资源软件有助于制定员工保留和招聘策略。 如果组织的流失率很高，人力资源软件将为人力资源经理提供必要的分析信息，以帮助他们克服问题。

分析和文档管理有助于实时分析数据并做出更好的决策。 一个好的 HR 软件解决方案包括安全传输和 SSL 协议，这些协议会在所有数据通过 Internet 传输时对其进行加密。

对于大多数新员工来说，与管理层的互动是令人恐惧的，这可能会让新员工对新公司感到困惑。 人力资源软件帮助员工和新员工熟悉所有入职要求，并允许新员工在一个平台上访问所有必要的雇主和组织信息。

就整体效率而言，跟踪每位员工的表现至关重要。 组织应该有一个人力资源软件来跟踪每个员工的详细绩效数据。

管理人力资源软件跟踪公司的所有员工福利，例如医疗保健、福利、税收减免和其他财务选择。

这对组织的日程安排有重大影响。 该模块不能独立运行，必须链接到另一个系统。 当 CRM（客户关系管理）和 ERP（企业资源规划）集成到 HR 软件中时，它就更有意义了。

人力资源软件旨在按照公司的政策工作。 该模块可用于自动安排休假。 安排、跟踪和批准任何类型的休假都很简单。

这些报告提供了更全面的整体绩效视图，允许制定适当的举措来提高绩效。

员工可以使用 HR 软件的关键元素检查和更新他们的个人信息，这减轻了 HR 专业人员的重大管理负担。 当员工有权访问和控制自己的信息时，可以增加他们的授权感和参与感。

员工姓名、电子邮件地址、状态、休假通知、生日、活动和假期日历都可以在线和离线使用。 只需查看仪表板，您就可以获得所需的所有信息。

该程序得到很好的保护； 遵循安全流程，以高精度生成代码和密码，以加密形式接收和传输数据，并定期更新系统以确保对系统没有威胁。

人力资源软件还简化了分配预算和跟踪培训成本的过程。



原文:

HR can be a complex department to manage. There are many responsibilities to navigate between hiring, scheduling, benefits, cross-compliance, etc.

What happens if an employee wants to see an old pay rise? How does anyone get information about their holidays or benefits?

Without the right technology, it will be a hassle for everyone involved.

This is why specialized HR software has become so popular. Companies of all sizes use these tools to improve efficiency and employee experience simultaneously.

Below mentioned are a few important software features:

1. Hiring & Recruitment

The technology may automate and optimize a data analysis of peer roles and propose the appropriate salary. With easy-to-prepare templates and recruiting follow-up, HR software automated recruitment modules speed up hiring procedures.

2. Portal for Employee

Financial data can be organized and stored using the Employee Portal HR software. Such a portal allows authorized users to access their information in real-time, saving HR time by eliminating the need to supervise such activities.

3. Payroll

Payroll's main job is to compute and pay salaries, withhold the necessary taxes and deductions, and coordinate the printing and distribution of paychecks.

4. Workflows

Workflows enable firms to automate the majority of the repetitive, routine procedures that stifle HR management productivity. To keep all participants informed.

5. Time tracking and entitlement to leave

Applicant Tracking System replaces the huge folders in closets where hundreds of resumes are filed. It automates finding people, enables you to assess the efficiency of various recruiting channels, and determines what works well enough and where more efforts and resources should be focused.

6. Evaluation of Performance

By holding KPAs and KRAs accountable, HR software effectively streamlines the evaluation process. It provides a more transparent and fair output of each employee's attendance and punctuality.

7. Employee Education

Every employee must upgrade their skill sets as technology and processes evolve. HR software assists in identifying this skill gap, aid in managing employees' training needs, and keeps track of the considerable costs associated with training and development.

8. Administration of Employee Benefits

HR specialists can use the system to create programs, set eligibility conditions, manage employee remuneration and promotions, and make payments or deposits to employees on top of their earnings. It also includes self-service open enrollment and accounting integration for compensation expenditures.

9. Retention of talent

HR software aids in the development of staff retention and recruitment strategies. If an organization's attrition rate is high, HR software will provide the HR Manager with essential analytical information to help them overcome the problem.

10. Data Management and Analytics

Analytics and document management aid in the real-time analysis of data and in making better decisions. A good HR software solution includes secure transmission and SSL protocols, which encrypt all data as it travels over the Internet.

11. Onboarding

Interacting with management is frightening for most new hires, which might leave a new employee perplexed about the new company. The HR Software assists staff and new hires become familiar with all onboarding requirements and allow new employees to access all necessary employer and organization information on one platform.

12. Management of Performance

When it comes to overall efficiency, it's critical to keep track of each employee's performance. Organizations should have an HR Software that keeps track of each employee's detailed performance data.

13. Administration

The Administration HR Software keeps track of all of a company's employee benefits, such as healthcare, welfare, tax breaks, and other financial alternatives.

14. Management of the Workforce

This has a significant impact on the organization's schedules. This module cannot function independently and must be linked to another system. When CRM (Customer Relationship Management) and ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) are integrated into HR Software, it makes more sense.

15. Leave and Absence

The HR software is designed to work in accordance with the company's policies. The module can be used to automate leave scheduling. It's simple to schedule, track, and approve any type of time off from work.

16. HR Analytics

HR Analytics generates reports by integrating with other HR Software and Other Systems modules. These reports provide a more comprehensive view of overall performance, allowing for the development of appropriate initiatives to improve performance.

17. Self-Service for Employees

Employees may examine and update their personal information using key elements of HR software, which relieves HR professionals of a significant administrative burden. When employees are given access to and control over their own information, it can increase their sense of empowerment and engagement.

18. Dashboard

Employees' names, email addresses, status, leave notifications, birthdays, events, and holiday calendars are all available online and offline. You can receive all the information you need just by looking at the dashboard.

19. Management of Safety

The program is well protected; a security process is followed, codes and passwords are produced with great precision, data is received and transmitted in encrypted form, and the system is updated on a regular basis to ensure that there is no threat to the system.

20. Development and Learning

The HR Software, which works in tandem with the performance management software, serves as a hub for personalized training, bookings, requirements, and feedback. The HR software also simplifies the process of allocating budgets and tracking training costs.
Items.Author: kpawan24

Items.Title: 您是否在 Mac 应用程序中使用 SwiftUI？ | Do you use SwiftUI for your Mac apps?
Items.Description:
加布里埃尔·雷默斯

发表于我们目前正在使用 SwiftUI 重写我们的闪存卡词汇学习应用 Wokabulary的 UI。 因此拥有一个独立于平台的 UI 系统可以为我们节省大量时间。

然而，我们在使用 SwiftUI 时面临不少挑战，尤其是在 Mac 上。 例如，Mac 上 SwiftUI 的性能通常比 AppKit 差（例如列表）。 此外，Mac 特定功能的文档非常稀少，有些事情无法完成（例如，可定制的工具栏）。

所以我们想知道：您是否在生产中为您的 Mac 应用程序使用 SwiftUI？ 您已经构建或正在使用 SwiftUI 构建哪些 Mac 应用程序？ 您面临哪些挑战？



原文:

Gabriel Reimers

Posted on May 23

We are currently re-writing the UI of our flash card vocabulary learning app Wokabulary with SwiftUI. Wokabulary is available for iPhone, iPad and Mac; so having a platform independent UI system saves us a lot of time.

However, we face quite a few challenges with SwiftUI especially on the Mac. For example performance of SwiftUI on the Mac is often worse than with AppKit (e.g. lists). Also the documentation for Mac specific features is very sparse and some things just cannot be done (e.g. customizable toolbars).

So we are wondering: Are you using SwiftUI for your Mac apps in production? What Mac apps have you built or are you building in SwiftUI? What challenges do you face?

Items.Title: Bearcam 伴侣：放大工作室 | Bearcam Companion: Amplify Studio
Items.Description:
我终于有机会在周末开始使用 Bearcam Companion 应用程序。 我在之前的帖子中描述了我的应用程序计划，我将我的最小可爱产品 (MLP) 定义如下：

  • 从网络摄像头抓取一帧
  • 检测框架中的熊（可能还有其他动物）
  • 在网页上显示框架和边界框
  • 允许用户（最终是 ML 模型）识别熊（并编辑检测错误）
  • 提供网页框架中每只熊的顶部标识

由于我最近没有做太多的 Web 应用程序工作，所以我决定从那里开始。 我看过一些关于使用Amplify CLI和各种前端框架开始使用AWS Amplify的教程。 为了快速启动和运行，我决定专注于后端并尝试了AWS Amplify Studio 。

有一些入门教程展示了如何使用各种前端框架构建基本的待办事项列表应用程序或网络博客。

数据模型

我首先定义了我需要的基本数据模型。 首先，我需要一个图像模型。 现在这可能是非常基本的。 我决定包含图像的 URL 和图像的日期（我可以用它来订购它们）。

一旦我有了图像，我计划使用对象检测模型来寻找熊。 我将需要存储找到的对象的信息，其中包括标签（例如“熊”）、置信度和边界框信息：宽度、高度、左侧 (x) 和顶部 (y)。 我称这个模型为Objects 。 由于一个图像可以有多个熊，我需要在Images和Objects之间创建 1:n 关系。

这是我的数据模型在 Amplify Studio 中的样子：

内容

我的模型中需要一些数据来测试我的应用程序。 我还没有准备好将图像添加到我的应用程序中（我还没有应用程序！），所以我将手动创建一些。 您可以使用虚拟数据自动创建数据，但我手动输入了一些。

图片

为了使我的数据尽可能真实，我使用了 Brooks Falls Brown Bears 的“探索快照”页面中的一些图像。 我通过复制快照中的 URL 和日期手动将 6 张图像添加到我的图像模型中：

对象

在编辑Images数据时，我也可以添加Objects ，但首先，我需要弄清楚对象数据的详细信息。 最简单的开始方法是使用Amazon Rekognition演示，因为Bear是公认的标签。 单击“开始使用 Amazon Rekognition”按钮，将您带到 AWS 控制台。 从那里，单击尝试演示按钮。 在演示页面上，您可以添加一个 URL 并获取响应：

响应以json形式提供，与对 Rekognition 的 API 调用返回的方式相同。 这是包含两只熊的图像的示例：

 { "Labels" : [ { "Name" : "Bear" , "Confidence" : 96.10771942138672 , "Instances" : [ { "BoundingBox" : { "Width" : 0.29982757568359375 , "Height" : 0.6181714534759521 , "Left" : 0.6161065697669983 , "Top" : 0.14535307884216309 }, "Confidence" : 96.10771942138672 }, { "BoundingBox" : { "Width" : 0.23141272366046906 , "Height" : 0.5379980206489563 , "Left" : 0.33176615834236145 , "Top" : 0.011607191525399685 }, "Confidence" : 72.4666976928711 } ] } ], "LabelModelVersion" : "2.0"
}

当我第一次实现熊检测的自动流程时，我可能会使用 Rekognition。 现在，我可以从 Rekognition 演示中获取响应结果，并将它们插入到每个图像的一个或多个对象中：

在对 6 个图像中的每一个执行此操作后，我的对象模型中最终有 12 个对象。

用户界面库

接下来，我致力于 UI 的设计元素。 此时我不需要任何特别的东西，所以我从示例组件开始，并将它们与我的 Amplify UI 库同步。

标准卡

我想在屏幕底部列出最近的图像。 我使用了从 Figma 示例中导入的 StandardCard，并将它们附加到 Amplify Studio 中的图像数据模型中。 我将图像连接到 images.url 并使用 images.data 作为文本组的第一行。 我在文本组中隐藏了另外两行，因为我不需要它们。 我的标准卡看起来像这样：

如您所见，Amplify Studio 正在显示带有来自我的数据模型的真实数据的 StandardCard。 您可以单击随机播放预览数据以查看卡片在不同数据下的外观，这非常方便。

帧集合

我想要多个 StandardCard 来显示最新的图像。 从 StandardCard 配置中，我可以单击Create collection 。 在集合配置中，我可以控制布局并添加搜索和分页等功能。 我从左到右选择了一个分页列表，页面大小为 4。我还设置了一个排序条件以首先查看最近的图像。 这是我的 FrameCollection 配置：

同样，组件显示来自我的数据模型的数据，包括排序条件和分页。

结论

这就是我现在在后端所需要的。 开始使用 AWS Amplify Studio 非常简单。 我建立了一个数据模型，将它连接到一个简单的 UI 组件 (StandardCard) 并创建并配置了一个集合 (FrameCollection)。 接下来我需要将后端配置拉到我的开发机器上并开始前端。

我会保存下一次...



原文:

I finally had a chance to start working on the Bearcam Companion app over the weekend. I had described my plans for the app in a previous post, where I defined my Minimum Lovable Product (MLP) as follows:

  • Grab a frame from the webcam
  • Detect the bears (and possibly other animals) in the frame
  • Display the frame and bounding boxes on a webpage
  • Allow users (and eventually an ML model) to identify the bears (and edit detection errors)
  • Provide the top identifications for each bear in the frame on the webpage

Since I haven't done much web application work recently, I decided to start there. I had watched a few tutorials on getting started with AWS Amplify using the Amplify CLI and various frontend frameworks. To get up and running quickly, I decided to focus on the backend and gave AWS Amplify Studio a try.

AWS Amplify provides a set of tools to make it easier to configure web services and connect them to a web frontend UI. AWS Amplify Studio is a visual interface to do many of the same things. There are some Getting Started tutorials which show how to build a basic todo list app or web blog using various front end frameworks.

Data Model

I started out by defining the basic data model I needed. As a start, I need an Images model. This can be pretty basic for now. I decided on including a URL to the image and the date for the image (which I can use to order them).

Once I have images, I plan to use an object detection model to find the bears. I will need to store the information for the found objects, which includes a label (e. g. 'Bear'), confidence level and the bounding box information: width, height, left (x) and top (y). I called this model, Objects. Since one image can have multiple bears, I need to create a 1:n relationship between Images and Objects.

This is what my data models look like in Amplify Studio:

Content

I will need some data in my models for testing my application. I'm not ready to implement adding images to my applications yet (I don't have an app yet!), so I'll create some manually. Amplify Studio has a Content manager to make this easier. You can create data automatically using dummy data, but I entered some manually.

Images

To keep my data as real as possible, I used some images from the Explore Snapshots page for the Brooks Falls Brown Bears. I manually added 6 images to my Images model by copying the URL and the date from the snapshot:

Objects

When editing the Images data, I can also add Objects, but first, I need to figure out the object data details. The easiest way to start was to use Amazon Rekognition demo, since Bear is a recognized label. Click on the Get Started with Amazon Rekognition button which takes you to the AWS Console. From there, click the Try Demo button. On the demo page, you can add a URL and get back the response:

The Response is provided in json, the same way that would be returned by an API call to Rekognition. Here's an example from an image which contained two bears:

{ "Labels": [ { "Name": "Bear", "Confidence": 96.10771942138672, "Instances": [ { "BoundingBox": { "Width": 0.29982757568359375, "Height": 0.6181714534759521, "Left": 0.6161065697669983, "Top": 0.14535307884216309 }, "Confidence": 96.10771942138672 }, { "BoundingBox": { "Width": 0.23141272366046906, "Height": 0.5379980206489563, "Left": 0.33176615834236145, "Top": 0.011607191525399685 }, "Confidence": 72.4666976928711 } ] } ], "LabelModelVersion": "2.0"
}

I may use Rekognition when I first implement an automatic flow for bear detection. For now, I can take the Response results from the Rekognition demo and plug them into one or more Objects for each of the Images:

After doing this for each of the 6 images, I ended up with 12 objects in my Objects model.

UI Library

Next I worked on the design elements for the UI. AWS provides some example UI elements developed in Figma, which you can duplicate and use in your projects. I don't need anything special at this point, so I started with the example components and synced them with my Amplify UI Library.

StandardCard

I want to have a list of recent images across the bottom of the screen. I used the StandardCard imported from the Figma examples and attached them to my Images data model in Amplify Studio. I connected the image to images.url and used images.data for the first line of the text group. I hid the other 2 lines in the text group since I don't need them. My StandardCard looks like this:

As you can see, Amplify Studio is showing the StandardCard with real data from my data model. You can click Shuffle preview data to see how the card looks with different data, which is pretty handy.

FrameCollection

I want to have multiple StandardCards to show the most recent images. From the StandardCard configuration I can click Create collection. In the collection configuration I can control the layout and add features like search and pagination. I chose a list from left to right with pagination and a page size of 4. I also set a sort condition to see the most recent images first. Here's my FrameCollection configuration:

Again, the component is show with data from my data model, including the sort condition and pagination.

Conclusion

That's all I need in the back end for now. It was quite simple to get started with AWS Amplify Studio. I set up a data model, connected it to a simple UI component (StandardCard) and created and configured a collection (FrameCollection). Next I need to pull the backend configuration to my development machine and get started on the front end.

I'll save that for next time...

Ever looked for a comprehensive intro to JDBC that is fun and entertaining at the same time? Then have a look at this brand-new episode of the "Marco Codes" YouTube channel: JDBC Tutorial - Nice & Easy.

In this video, you'll learn how to use JDBC, i.e. the basic API that every Java program uses to access databases:

Items.Title: Hire +Plus 的自我记录：V1 (2) | Self-documentation of Hire +Plus: V1 (2)
Items.Description:
在我深入创建这个应用程序的主要部分之前，我想从小处着手并逐步建立。 我很早就添加了 typescript 和 cypress 测试以尽早开始。

添加打字稿

由于这是一个现有项目，我需要单独安装 typescript。

首先：这将安装安装 typescript 所需的一切
npm install --save typescript @types/node @types/react @types/react-dom @types/jest

第二：在根目录中创建一个tsconfig.json文件并粘贴它。 这是一个基本的打字稿设置，可以让一切正常工作。

 { "compilerOptions": { "target": "es5", "lib": ["dom", "dom.iterable", "esnext"], "downlevelIteration": true, "allowJs": true, "skipLibCheck": true, "esModuleInterop": true, "allowSyntheticDefaultImports": true, "strict": true, "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true, "noFallthroughCasesInSwitch": true, "module": "esnext", "moduleResolution": "node", "resolveJsonModule": true, "isolatedModules": true, "noEmit": true, "jsx": "react-jsx" }, "include": ["src"] }

最后：我将 react 组件扩展从.js/.jsx转换为 typescript 扩展（ .ts/tsx ）。

将组件作为 Typescript 启动

这里似乎没有太大变化，但我将组件的扩展名更改为.tsx并将其转换为 typescript。 简单的过渡，但随着我的进行，它会变得更加复杂。

修复index.tsx文件中的错误

转换Launch组件时出现此错误。 我发现（通过谷歌搜索）打字稿需要确保 id 为 root 的元素将在dom中，因为它可能为空。

解决方案是添加一个！ 抓取根元素时。 我确保总会有一个 id 为 root 的元素
现在我们修复了该错误，我们正式配置并运行了 typescript。 让我们进入测试。

将 Cypress 与 React 一起使用

我将使用 cypress 来运行测试。 设置很简单（我很高兴！）。 赛普拉斯有自己的仪表板和环境来运行测试。 第一：使用 npm 安装 cypress
npm install cypress --save-dev

二：打通cypress环境
npx cypress open

会发生两件事，

  1. 在项目文件夹中，使用相同的示例测试创建了一个 cypress 文件夹。 这是您编写测试的地方。

最后，我编写了一个简单的测试来确保启动页面正确呈现。 我在集成文件夹中创建了一个launch.spec.js文件。

添加这个测试后，我回到我的测试环境，我可以看到它通过了。

如果此代码看起来令人困惑，我建议您访问 cypress 网站以进一步了解详细信息。

暂时就这些了，更多精彩敬请期待！

源代码



原文:

Before I dive into creating the major parts of this app, I want to start small and build up. I added typescript and cypress testing early to get an early start.

Adding Typescript

Since this is an existing project, I need to install typescript separately.

First: This will install everything you need to get typescript installed
npm install --save typescript @types/node @types/react @types/react-dom @types/jest

Second: create a tsconfig.json file in the root dir and paste this. It is a basic typescript setup to get everything working.

{
    "compilerOptions": {
        "target": "es5",
        "lib": ["dom", "dom.iterable", "esnext"],
        "downlevelIteration": true,
        "allowJs": true,
        "skipLibCheck": true,
        "esModuleInterop": true,
        "allowSyntheticDefaultImports": true,
        "strict": true,
        "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
        "noFallthroughCasesInSwitch": true,
        "module": "esnext",
        "moduleResolution": "node",
        "resolveJsonModule": true,
        "isolatedModules": true,
        "noEmit": true,
        "jsx": "react-jsx"
    },
    "include": ["src"]
}

Lastly: I converted the react component extension from .js/.jsx to typescript extension (.ts/tsx).

Launch Component as Typescript

Does not seem like much of a change here, but I changed the extension of the component to .tsx and converted it to typescript. The const Launch: React.FunctionalComponent = () => ensures this components type is a functional component with no props. Simple transition, but it will get more complex as I go along.

Fix error in index.tsx file

I got this error when I converted the Launch component. I discovered (with googling) that typescript needed to be sure the element with the id of root was going to be in the dom because it might be null.

The solution was to add an ! when grabbing the root element. I'm ensuring that there will always be an element with an id of root
link to solution on stackoverflow

Now that we fixed that error, we officially have typescript configured and working. Let's get into testing.

Using Cypress with React

I'll be using cypress to run tests. Setting it up was simple (I'm glad!). Cypress has its dashboard and environment to run tests. Cypress can run unit, integration, and end-to-end tests with cypress.

First: Installing cypress with npm
npm install cypress --save-dev

Second: Open up the cypress environment
npx cypress open

Two things will happen,

  1. the cypress environment will open, and you'll see sample tests in the integration folder.

  2. in the project folder, a cypress folder is created with the same sample test. This is where you'll write your tests at.

Lastly, I wrote a simple test to make sure the launch page is rendering correctly. I created a launch.spec.js file inside the integration folder.

Code inside the launch.spec.js file.

after adding this test, I went back to my testing environment, I can see that it passed.

I suggest going to the cypress website to look further into the details if this code seems confusing.

P.S. I suggest using this VScode extension to help with react typescript snippets (very helpful).

That's all for now, stay tuned for more!

source code
